evals.prompt: Resolve review comments on PR #42. Fix the code, verify the branch, reply to the reviewers, and resolve the open threads after pushing.
evals.expected_output: Processes unresolved review threads, applies the required fixes, runs verification, pushes the branch, replies to the comments, and resolves the threads.
expectations: Mentions collecting unresolved review threads and their thread IDs
Mentions reading the referenced code before changing it
Mentions running verification such as build, typecheck, lint, or tests
Mentions replying to the review comments and resolving the threads
Mentions pushing the branch after the fixes are verified

evals.prompt: I only gave you PR number 46. How should the resolve-pr skill figure out which repository that belongs to and avoid making assumptions?
evals.expected_output: Detects the repository from git remotes instead of assuming origin or a hardcoded owner/repo pair.
expectations: States that the remote name must be detected rather than assumed
Mentions deriving owner/repo from the actual configured remote URL

evals.prompt: One review comment is just asking why we kept the existing implementation. What should the resolve-pr skill do if no code change is needed?
evals.expected_output: Replies with a grounded explanation and resolves the thread only after confirming that no code change is required.
expectations: States that a code change is not always required
Mentions replying with rationale when the implementation is already correct
Does not suggest changing code just to silence the comment
